stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: WWDC 2026 bonus live blog: Tech Talk with Craig Federighi
stories.author: Nilay Patel
bullet_points: The content focuses on a technical deep dive into the architecture enabling Apple Intelligence.
The discussion features Craig Federighi, Apple SVP of Software Engineering, indicating high-level insight.
Apple Intelligence is positioned as the core feature driving nearly all upcoming updates.

stories.title: Apple announces Siri AI and its next generation of Apple Intelligence
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Apple unveiled a new, significantly upgraded Siri AI at WWDC.
The new Siri is positioned as a more conversational and capable AI assistant.
This marks the realization of Apple's previously announced Apple Intelligence plans.

stories.title: For the 2nd time in weeks, Microsoft packages laced with credential stealer
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a recurring security vulnerability involving credential stealer packages.
The malicious packages are designed to execute self-replicating code upon being opened by an AI agent.
This highlights a critical and growing security risk associated with AI agent interactions with software dependencies.

stories.title: How AI Agents Reshape Knowledge Work: Autonomy, Efficiency, and Scope
stories.author: Jerry Ma
bullet_points: Frontier AI is evolving from conversational assistants to autonomous agents capable of end-to-end task execution.
The research empirically examines how these AI agents are accelerating and reshaping knowledge work.
The study utilizes production data from Perplexity's Search and Computer products to draw key findings.

stories.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
stories.author: unclefuzzy
bullet_points: Apple is integrating AI capabilities by building architecture around Google Gemini models.
This signals a significant strategic partnership between two tech giants in the AI space.
The development suggests a major shift in how Apple plans to deploy advanced generative AI features.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
It utilizes key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI execution on Mac hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is enabling AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Apple announces watchOS 27, now with Siri AI
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Apple has announced watchOS 27, the next iteration of the Apple Watch operating system.
The key new feature is the integration of Siri AI capabilities into the watchOS.
The update will also feature a redesigned 'dynamic' app grid and enhanced health tracking, rolling out this fall.

stories.title: Cameras get an Apple Intelligence boost in Apple Home
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Apple Intelligence is being integrated into HomeKit Secure Video for cameras.
The AI will analyze footage and generate descriptive summaries of recorded events.
Users will gain the ability to search camera footage using natural language queries.

stories.title: Apple announces visionOS 27 with Siri AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Apple unveiled visionOS 27 at WWDC, bringing updates to its ecosystem.
The new OS features an updated Siri AI assistant integrated across platforms.
visionOS 27 enables users to interact with Siri using visual intelligence while wearing the Vision Pro.

stories.title: Say hi to "Siri AI"—Apple announces new, more "conversational" voice assistant
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Apple is introducing a new, more 'conversational' voice assistant, branded as 'Siri AI'.
The update will feature new capabilities rolling out this fall.
The overhaul involves a two-tiered AI model powered by Google.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: MemDreamer addresses the limitations of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) when processing very long videos due to token explosion and attention dilution.
The core innovation is decoupling perception and reasoning by framing long-video understanding as an agentic exploration process.
It utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory with a three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction to manage video information incrementally.

stories.title: Act As a Real Researcher: A Suite of Benchmarks Evaluating Frontier LLMs and Agentic Harnesses in Research Lifecycle
stories.author: Xiangyong Cao
bullet_points: New benchmarks evaluate frontier LLMs and agentic harnesses across the entire research lifecycle.
Agents show high proficiency in complex coding and autonomous experiment execution.
Current agents lack critical capabilities in field sensitivity, ethics, and nuanced scientific judgment, limiting their replacement of human researchers.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of token explosion and attention dilution in processing long-form video using Vision-Language Models.
Introduces MemDreamer, a framework that decouples perception from reasoning to enable long-video understanding.
Utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory and an agentic retrieval mechanism for semantic abstraction across streamed video content.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Apple drops support for a long list of iPads and Apple Watches with latest OS updates
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Apple is discontinuing software support for several older iPad and Apple Watch models with the release of the latest OS updates (watchOS 27 and iPadOS 27).
The company is reportedly dropping support for four generations of Apple devices, indicating an accelerated pace of hardware obsolescence.
This trend highlights Apple's practice of phasing out older hardware with new software releases, impacting users of legacy devices.

stories.title: Apple WWDC 2026: The 7 biggest announcements
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple introduced an AI-upgraded Siri at WWDC 2026.
The new Siri promises more personalized assistance capabilities.
The event also showcased various updates across Apple's operating systems.

stories.title: Apple is redesigning Screen Time and overhauling child controls
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Apple is significantly redesigning its Screen Time parental control features.
The overhaul aims to enhance safeguarding measures for children using Apple devices (iPhone, iPad, Mac).
New features are slated to arrive with the iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27 updates.

stories.title: Amazon is launching AI-generated custom merch
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: Amazon is integrating AI-generated designs into its print-on-demand service.
Users can create custom merchandise (T-shirts, hoodies, etc.) using text prompts via Alexa for Shopping.
The feature allows users to generate, share, and sell their AI-designed products on Amazon.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google NotebookLM is receiving a significant upgrade with the integration of Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity.
The advanced features are currently restricted to AI Ultra and enterprise subscription accounts.
This signals Google's push to integrate its latest large language models into productivity tools.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs' probabilistic reasoning by testing them on discrete probability problems.
Models perform very well (0.96 accuracy) on standard probability exercises.
Performance drops significantly (0.59 accuracy) when models are tested on counterintuitive problems, suggesting limitations in heuristic reasoning.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, a major challenge in AI.
Proposes SETA (Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning) to mitigate catastrophic forgetting.
The framework distinguishes between task-specific knowledge and shared capabilities using adaptive sparse experts.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: HPC clusters are transitioning from traditional deterministic pipelines to new AI-driven workflows.
AI integration introduces a new computational paradigm characterized by iterative, data-driven, and probabilistic processes.
The shift presents unique technical challenges for designing and managing modern scientific computing workflows.

stories.title: Whisper Hallucination Detection and Mitigation via Hidden Representation Steering and Sparse AutoEncoders
stories.author: Assel Yermekova
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of 'hallucinations' in the widely used Whisper ASR model.
Proposes detecting and mitigating these hallucinations by analyzing Whisper's internal representations (raw activations and SAE latents).
Demonstrates that both raw activations and Sparse AutoEncoder (SAE) latents successfully encode hallucination-related information.

stories.title: Planning-aligned Token Compression for Long-Context Autonomous Driving
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of monolithic vision-action models in autonomous driving when handling long temporal contexts.
Proposes 'Planning-aligned Token Compression' as a method to reduce token sequence size without modifying the core model architecture.
Highlights that existing compression methods are often rule-based, suggesting a need for more integrated solutions.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, a major challenge in AI.
Introduces SETA (Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning) to mitigate catastrophic forgetting.
The framework differentiates between task-specific knowledge and shared capabilities using adaptive sparse experts.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: HPC clusters are shifting from traditional deterministic pipelines to AI-driven workflows.
AI integration introduces new computational paradigms characterized by iterative, data-driven, and probabilistic processes.
The transition presents unique technical challenges for designing and optimizing these new workflows.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs' probabilistic reasoning using controlled dice-rolling problems.
Models perform highly on standard probability exercises (0.96 accuracy) but struggle with counterintuitive problems (0.59 accuracy).
The research evaluates 8 state-of-the-art models, testing the impact of Chain-of-Thought prompting.

stories.title: TEVI: Text-Conditioned Editing of Visual Representations via Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Vision-Language Alignment
stories.author: Bernt Schiele
bullet_points: Proposes TEVI, a framework to improve vision-language alignment in models like CLIP.
Addresses the information imbalance where images contain more data than their text captions describe.
Uses captions as a guiding signal to selectively retain relevant information from image embeddings via Sparse Autoencoders.

stories.title: Sycophantic Praise: Evaluating Excessive Praise in Language Models
stories.author: Tianyu Jiang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap in current LLM alignment research by focusing specifically on 'sycophantic praise' rather than just excessive agreement.
It proposes a new parameterized framework to quantify whether praise given by an LLM is excessive relative to the actual quality of the contribution and user ability.
The research suggests that sycophantic praise is a distinct alignment problem that existing measurement methods fail to capture accurately.

stories.title: The Lipreading Gap: Do VSR Models Perceive Visual Speech Like Human Lipreaders?
stories.author: Naomi Harte
bullet_points: VSR models are outperforming human lipreaders on current benchmarks, but the study questions if this equates to human-like perception.
The research compares three VSR systems against human baselines across word, character, phoneme, and viseme levels on the MaFI dataset.
Despite higher overall accuracy, the models and humans exhibit different strengths and weaknesses when recognizing specific words.

stories.title: The Masked Advantage: Uncovering Local-Language Access to Cultural Knowledge in LLMs
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Investigates whether local cultural knowledge in LLMs is better accessed via English or the local language.
Identifies limitations in current evaluations, such as reliance on rigid, template-based questions.
Proposes new methods to disentangle general language proficiency from language-conditioned knowledge access.

stories.title: M$^3$Exam: Benchmarking Multimodal Memory for Realistic User-Agent Interactions
stories.author: Xiaofang Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces M$^3$Exam, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal conversational memory in language agents.
Addresses limitations in current benchmarks by focusing on realistic user-agent interactions rather than idealized human-human dialogue.
Evaluates agents on complex tasks, including reasoning over authentic multimodal file interaction and interpreting concealed user information.

stories.title: The agent that fixes bugs by running the code
stories.author: shreyshah
bullet_points: The article discusses shifting focus from improving AI models to harnessing engineering practices in AI-assisted coding.
It introduces the concept of a 'runtime evidence loop' as a mechanism for turning AI guesses into functional code fixes.
The core argument is that engineering rigor, not just model sophistication, represents the next major frontier in AI development for coding.
